isolation.notes: Validated only the assigned browser assertions against the already-running local app. The dev server was not restarted, no application code was modified, and the agent-browser session was closed after evidence capture.

assertionResults.title: Cards are near-transparent glass over shader
assertionResults.reason: The glass treatment itself is present across overview, tokenomics, roadmap, and architecture surfaces, but sampled BetterCard surfaces render a ~14px corner radius instead of the required 8px radius.

steps.action: Inspect the default graph workspace card grid and the first-view supporting cards at rest.
steps.expected: Cards should read as near-transparent glass panels over the blue shader, with 4% white fill, 12% white borders, no backdrop blur, and an 8px radius.
steps.observed: The overview cards clearly show the shader through them and compute to `rgba(255,255,255,0.04)` backgrounds with `1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` borders and no backdrop blur, but the sampled cards render `border-radius: 14px`.

steps.action: Inspect a representative tokenomics surface card and architecture stack card.
steps.expected: Detail surfaces should preserve the same transparent glass styling over the shader background.
steps.observed: The tokenomics Base Contract card and architecture stack cards keep the same transparent glass base (`rgba(255,255,255,0.04)`), subtle border, and no blur while the shader remains visible behind the content. Their runtime radius also resolves to `14px`.

steps.expected: Roadmap detail cards should match the same glass-over-shader treatment without becoming opaque boxes.
steps.observed: Roadmap detail cards keep the transparent shader-visible look and the same 0.04/0.12 styling, but again render with `border-radius: 14px` rather than the contract's 8px target.

evidence.network: No requests were captured during this local visual validation flow.

assertionResults.issues: Strict contract mismatch on corner radius: sampled shadcn cards are transparent glass over the shader, but they are more rounded than the required 8px.

assertionResults.title: All text white with size/weight hierarchy
assertionResults.reason: Representative body copy stays white or opacity-based white, but the primary `SEE WHAT'S LIVE` CTA still renders dark text (`rgb(16,16,16)`), so not every text element is white under the strict shadcn assertion.

steps.action: Inspect overview body copy, graph-card copy, and representative tokenomics/architecture detail text in browser.
steps.expected: All text should render as white or opacity-based white with hierarchy from size, weight, and opacity instead of grey text colors.
steps.observed: Overview, tokenomics, and architecture text samples resolved to white/opacity-white values such as `rgb(255,255,255)`, `lab(98.26 0 0)`, and `oklab(... / 0.7)` or `oklab(... / 0.8)`. No grey body text was observed in the sampled detail surfaces.

steps.action: Inspect the primary CTA text styling in the first viewport.
steps.expected: The assertion requires every text element to stay white.
steps.observed: `SEE WHAT'S LIVE` computes to `color: rgb(16, 16, 16)` on a white background, while the adjacent `EXPLORE THE ATLAS` CTA remains white text on a transparent background.

steps.action: Cross-check other representative detail surfaces for non-white text.
steps.expected: Representative tokenomics and architecture surfaces should not introduce grey or dark text.
steps.observed: Tokenomics and architecture focus states showed only white/opacity-white text in the sampled visible nodes; the dark-text finding was limited to the overview white CTA.

evidence.network: No requests were captured during this local visual validation flow.

assertionResults.issues: The primary white CTA still uses dark text, so the strict all-text-white rule is not fully met even though representative body/detail copy no longer appears grey.

assertionResults.title: Proper margins, padding, and spacing
assertionResults.reason: Representative overview, roadmap, tokenomics, and architecture cards show consistent padding and spacing with no overlap or zero-gap adjacency.

steps.action: Inspect the overview graph workspace card grid.
steps.expected: Primary cards should have at least `p-3`-level internal padding and consistent spacing between adjacent cards.
steps.observed: Overview cards render at `12px` padding on all sides with `12px` card-internal gaps, and the first-view grid shows consistent spacing between adjacent cards without overlap.

steps.action: Inspect representative detail surfaces in roadmap, tokenomics, and architecture.
steps.expected: Detail cards should preserve coherent spacing and larger primary-card padding while compact subcards may stay smaller.
steps.observed: Roadmap detail cards use `20px` padding with `16px` gaps, architecture stack cards use `16px` padding with `16px` gaps, and the tokenomics Base Contract card uses compact `8px 12px` padding appropriate for a smaller info card inside the larger tokenomics surface.

steps.action: Visually check for awkward collisions or zero-margin adjacency across representative surfaces.
steps.expected: No cards should collide, overlap, or visually collapse into each other.
steps.observed: Across the sampled overview grid and detail panels, card spacing remains visually consistent and readable; no overlap, clipping, or zero-margin adjacency was observed.

evidence.network: No requests were captured during this local visual validation flow.

assertionResults.title: Smooth transitions with no jank
assertionResults.reason: Hovered cards transition on `0.2s ease` with unchanged bounding boxes and no visible flicker or layout shift in the sampled overview and roadmap-detail interactions.

steps.action: Hover the overview Tokenomics card and compare its box metrics before and after hover.
steps.expected: Hover should animate smoothly without changing layout geometry or causing flicker.
steps.observed: The overview Tokenomics card stayed at `314 x 145.33` before and after hover while its visual state changed over `0.2s ease`; no jump or layout shift was observed.

steps.action: Hover a larger roadmap detail card and inspect the runtime transition.
steps.expected: The cursor-tracking sheen should follow smoothly without visible stutter or broken animation timing.
steps.observed: The `AUTONOMOUS STRATEGY AGENTS` roadmap card hovered cleanly with the same `0.2s ease` timing and stable layout; the sheen tracked the pointer position and no flicker or collapsed state was seen in the session.

steps.action: Check the browser runtime log after the hover flows.
steps.expected: No console/runtime errors should accompany the transition checks.
steps.observed: No console errors were reported after the hover validation flow.

evidence.network: No requests were captured during this local visual validation flow.

hoverSubtletyRecheck.title: Cursor-tracking metallic sheen on shadcn cards
hoverSubtletyRecheck.reason: The hover sheen is still far brighter than the updated subtlety target. Sampled cards use a `rgba(255,255,255,0.38)` center highlight plus a `rgba(200,210,255,0.12)` secondary ring, and the resulting hotspot becomes one of the brightest elements on the page.

steps.action: Hover the overview Tokenomics card in the default graph workspace.
steps.expected: The sheen should be restrained and supportive, with a center highlight around 0.12-0.18 max and no sharp brightening under the cursor.
steps.observed: The hovered Tokenomics card showed a large bright white hotspot that noticeably brightened the upper-left portion of the card, reading as attention-grabbing rather than barely-there.

steps.action: Hover a roadmap detail card (`AUTONOMOUS STRATEGY AGENTS`) and inspect the result on a larger surface.
steps.expected: The larger card should keep the same subtle metallic response without flare-like hotspots.
steps.observed: The roadmap detail card produced an even broader bright hotspot across the card face. The card remained smooth, but the sheen was visually dominant rather than quiet.

steps.action: Inspect the hovered cards' computed styles.
steps.expected: The contract target is roughly `rgba(255,255,255,0.12)` to `0.18` max for the center highlight and `rgba(200,210,255,0.04-0.08)` for the ring.
steps.observed: Both sampled hovers resolved to `background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` with `background-image: radial-gradient(... rgba(255,255,255,0.38) 0%, rgba(200,210,255,0.12) 40%, transparent 70%)` plus `inset 0 0 30px rgba(255,255,255,0.03)`, which exceeds the updated subtlety targets.

evidence.network: No requests were captured during this local visual validation flow.

relatedVisualExpectations.note: The base glass-morphism is present, but the hover sheen becomes brighter than the surrounding text/shader instead of staying restrained and refined.
Cards remain transparent enough for the shader to show through, but the hover response still brightens sharply under the cursor rather than complementing the background quietly.

hoverSubtletyRecheck.issues: The current BetterCard hover effect is smooth but still too bright and attention-grabbing to satisfy the softened hover-subtlety requirement.

summary: Validated the assigned shadcn browser surfaces across the overview workspace plus roadmap, tokenomics, and architecture detail states. Primary assertions: VAL-SHADCN-006 and VAL-SHADCN-007 passed; VAL-SHADCN-003 failed on the strict 8px-radius requirement despite the correct glass transparency; VAL-SHADCN-005 failed because the primary white CTA still renders dark text. Additional revalidation for VAL-SHADCN-004 failed: the hover sheen remains smooth but still uses a bright 0.38 white hotspot / 0.12 ring and would miss the subtle-supportive hover expectations behind VAL-VISUAL-030 and VAL-VISUAL-035.
